senses.definition: a praise or encouragement given to someone because of what they have done or achieved

examples.x: The coach gave the team a pat on the back for their outstanding performance in the championship game.
She always offered her friends a pat on the back when they achieved their goals, no matter how big or small.
The teacher's encouraging words and a pat on the back motivated the student to continue striving for excellence.

senses.definition: to praise or encourage someone to show one thinks they have done a great job at doing something

examples.x: After his stellar performance in the school play, the teacher couldn't help but pat him on the back and commend his acting skills.
When her friend passed the difficult exam, she eagerly patted her on the back to celebrate the accomplishment.

senses.definition: approval of a person in authority that a person tries to gain by doing something that pleases or impresses them

examples.x: She scored brownie points with her in-laws by baking them their favorite cookies for their visit.
Offering to do the dishes after the family dinner earned him some brownie points with his parents.
The employee hoped that volunteering for the charity event would earn him brownie points with his boss.
Complimenting the boss's presentation was a clever way to gain a few brownie points at the office.
Helping her neighbor carry groceries upstairs was a simple act of kindness that earned her some brownie points in the building.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv kɹˈɛdɪt wˌɛɹ kɹˈɛdɪt ɔːɹ ɪt ɪz dˈuː

senses.definition: used for saying that one should acknowledge and appreciate someone's achievements or efforts when they rightfully deserve recognition
to treat someone with proper care and respect

examples.x: The company's policy of doing well by customers through excellent service led to a loyal customer base.

senses.definition: to praise or admire someone because of what they have done or achieved

examples.x: I have to take my hat off to her for her incredible dedication to charity work.

word_list.pronunciation: sˈɪŋ ðə pɹˈeɪzᵻz ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to speak or write about someone or something in a very positive and enthusiastic way

examples.x: The critics sang the praises of the new play, calling it a masterpiece.
The teacher sang the praises of her students for their hard work on the project.
The politician sang the praises of the new tax cuts, claiming they would boost the economy.
The restaurant owner sang the praises of her chef, saying he was the best in town.
The musician sang the praises of her bandmates for their talent and dedication.

senses.definition: said as a way of showing praise or appreciation for someone or inviting other people to clap for them
to treat a guest whom one greatly values with extra care and attention

examples.x: When the celebrity arrived at the movie premiere, the organizers rolled out the red carpet for a grand entrance.
The university rolled out the red carpet for the Nobel laureate, arranging a special ceremony in their honor.
The restaurant staff rolled out the red carpet for the food critic, offering a personalized dining experience.
The company spared no expense in rolling out the red carpet for their most important clients at the annual gala.
At the international summit, the host country went all out to roll out the red carpet for world leaders, ensuring a warm and dignified welcome.
